topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on promoting renewable energy sources and reducing emissions through various incentives and regulations.

Document 1: study of black carbon emissions
recommendations regarding areas of focus for additional research
Document 2: modifies the definition of qualifying income for publicly traded partnerships
transportation, storage, or marketing of any fuel
Document 3: tax credit for the purchase of new or retread tires made from recycled rubber
Document 4: tax credit for new qualified fuel cell motor vehicles
increases the amount of such credit for off-highway vehicles
Document 5: require a reduction in the income and excise tax credits for alcohol used for fuel
ensure that at least 50% of 2013 and 2014 model year automobiles are dual fueled
Document 6: establish a competitive grant program for renewable and alternative energy fueling infrastructure projects
projects must be capable of dispensing fuel or energy not widely available
Document 7: repeal provisions prohibiting any federal agency from entering into a contract for procurement of an alternative or synthetic fuel
lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions associated with the production and combustion of the fuel must be less than or equal to such emissions

topic.name: Environmental Land Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving land, water, and habitat management for ecological sustainability and community benefit.

Document 1: authorizes stewardship contracting projects with private persons or entities to achieve land management goals
land management goals may include watershed restoration and maintenance
Document 2: authorizes cooperative agreements and contracts for forest, rangeland, and watershed restoration
includes activities to restore or improve forest, rangeland, and watershed health
Document 3: technical assistance to enable small public water systems to comply with national primary drinking water regulations
Document 4: authorizes the Department of the Interior to amend the Definite Plan Report for the Seedskadee Project
provides for the study, design, planning, and construction activities for water management
Document 5: amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to define 'fill material' for water body management
Document 6: requires the establishment of a program to provide loans for habitat conservation plans
gives preference to subdivisions located in biologically rich regions for sustainable infrastructure development
Document 7: directs the Secretary of the Army to carry out hurricane and storm damage reduction projects
involves federal responsibility for operation and maintenance of navigation and floodgate features

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that amend the Social Security Act to improve and expand Medicare and Medicaid services.

Document 1: Seniors Mental Health Access Improvement Act of 2015
cover marriage and family therapist services and mental health counselor services under Medicare
Document 2: Medicare Adult Day Services Act of 2013
cover certified adult day services furnished in a certified adult day services center
Document 3: Medicare Advantage benchmark adjustment
for certain local areas with Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical centers
Document 4: Medicare Prescription Drug Price Negotiation Act of 2007
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers
Document 5: Medicare Oral Health Rehabilitative Enhancement Act of 2011
permit physical therapy to be furnished under the Medicare program to individuals under the care of a dentist
Document 6: Medicare Beneficiary Preservation of Choice Act of 2015
restore the option under previous law to elect to change from a MedicareAdvantage to the original Medicare fee-for-service plan
Document 7: Medicaid Accountability and Care Act of 2013
revise payments to states under the Medicaid program

topic.name: Reproductive Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts related to women's reproductive rights, including abortion, health care coverage, and legal protections.

Document 1: every woman has the fundamental right to choose to: (1) bear a child; (2) terminate a pregnancy prior to fetal viability
Prohibits a federal, state, or local governmental entity from denying or interfering with a woman's right to exercise such choices
Document 4: prohibit any person from performing or attempting to perform an abortion within the District of Columbia except in conformity with this Act's requirements
Makes an exception where necessary to save the life of a pregnant woman whose life is endangered
Document 7: require the Director of the Office of Personnel Management to ensure that no multi-state qualified health plan offered in a health benefit exchange provides coverage of abortion
Excepts from such limitation: (1) a pregnancy that results from rape or incest

topic.name: Housing and Community Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at providing housing assistance and support services to specific populations, including veterans, Native Americans, and low-income individuals.

Document 1: foreign nongovernmental organizations shall not be ineligible for assistance under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961
shall not be subject to requirements relating to the use of non-U.S. government funds for advocacy and lobbying activities
Document 2: establish a pilot program to award grants to nonprofit organizations that primarily serve veterans or low-income individuals
rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans
Document 3: extend through FY2015 the authorization of appropriations for housing assistance for Native Americans
make the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands of the state of Hawaii eligible for loan guarantees
Document 4: revise requirements for the administrative fee payable to public housing agencies
support to or reward family self-sufficiency programs that are particularly innovative or highly successful
Document 5: require the Department of Health and Human Services to establish a demonstration program to support tribal early childhood education
HHS must make grants to demonstration projects to build or upgrade facilities for early childhood care
Document 6: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to consult with state and local officials regarding the facility's location
state and local review of the proposed contract or grant
Document 7: allow a student who is a homeless child or youth to occupy low-income housing units
with respect to the low-income housing tax credit

topic.name: Legislative Actions and Environmental Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to environmental regulations, public health, and government operations.

Document 1: Directs the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to implement the Clean Air Interstate Rule (CAIR)
nothing in this Act shall change or modify the authority or obligations set forth in the Clean Air Act.
Document 6: authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to extend by six years the time period during which Wilkesboro Hydroelectric Company is required to commence construction
FERC must reinstate the construction license if it is expired.
Document 7: compliance with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (NEPA) does not require consideration of climate change effects or greenhouse gas emissions
prohibits the provisions of any analysis relating to climate change effects or greenhouse gas emissions from being the basis for any enforceable measure or action under any other provision of law.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at protecting consumers from fraud, misleading practices, and ensuring transparency in services and products.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to disseminate to seniors and their families information on fraud targeting seniors.
Directs the Attorney General to establish and administer a competitive grant program for fraud prevention education programs for senior citizens.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit third-party charges not related to telephone services without consumer consent.
Subjects violators to civil forfeiture and specified penalties and refund requirements.
Document 3: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promote nationwide broadband competition through wireless services.
Requires licensees to offer consumers a data service that is faster than 200 kilobits per second without subscription charges.
Document 4: Requires states to maintain beneficial ownership information for corporations to prevent misuse affecting interstate or foreign commerce.
Prescribes penalties for providing false beneficial ownership information.
Document 5: Directs the Commissioner of Social Security to issue uniform standards for the protection of Social Security numbers from fraud or identity theft.
Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit the display of Social Security account numbers on the Internet.
Document 6: Prohibits shipping or distributing video games without an age-based content rating label.
Treats violations of such requirements as an unfair or deceptive act subject to civil penalties.
Document 7: Requires retail distributors to display consumer alerts regarding digital television transition information.
Authorizes the FCC to issue civil forfeitures for violations of digital television consumer education requirements.

topic.name: Financial Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions, particularly in banking, mortgage lending, and payday loans.

Document 1: prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits
prescribes leverage ratio requirements for operating subsidiaries
Document 2: requires each servicer of a home mortgage to respond in writing within 75 days
authorizes an aggrieved individual to bring a civil action for damages
Document 3: requires a creditor to make specified disclosures when making a payday loan
prohibits a creditor from making a payday loan to any consumer unless specified notices are posted
Document 4: modifies the eligibility criteria for insurance of home equity conversion mortgages
authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to use a portion of reverse mortgage insurance premiums
Document 5: amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 relating to prohibitions on proprietary trading
prohibits any construction of these prohibitions to require divestiture of collateralized debt obligations
Document 6: establishes special rules for modification or disposition of a troubled asset under TARP
directs the Secretary of the Treasury to establish and implement a home mortgage loan relief program
Document 7: authorizes a mortgagor to file a motion before a court to stay any foreclosure proceedings
requires the court to enter an order adjusting the principal amount to the property's fair market value

topic.name: Federal Research and Development Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to establish and coordinate federal research initiatives across various scientific and technological fields.

Document 1: establishes a National Forensic Science Research Initiative
coordinate with relevant federal agencies in the development of a unified federal research strategy
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Energy to establish a National Bioenergy Partnership
provide coordination among programs of state governments, the federal government, and the private sector
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of State to work to advance the goals of the Global Alliance for Clean Cookstoves
authorizes appropriations for various federal agencies to work with the Alliance
Document 4: Establishes within the Department of Agriculture the National Institute of Food and Agriculture
Directs the Institute to provide grants to support and promote the highest quality of fundamental agricultural research
Document 5: Directs the President to create a National Intermodal Transportation Planning Task Force
draft a National Intermodal Transportation plan that includes short- and long-term goals
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to increase the number of Customs and Border Protection officers
Authorizes appropriations to make improvements to existing U.S. ports of entry
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish a highway emergency responders safety grant program
Authorizes the Secretary to make grants to nonprofit and not-for-profit organizations

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the legislative designations of various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals or entities.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1343 West Irving Park Road in Chicago, Illinois, as the 'Steve Goodman Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 787 State Route 17M in Monroe, New York, as the 'National Clandestine Service of the Central Intelligence Agency NCS Officer Gregg David Wenzel Memorial Post Office.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 101 West Main Street in Waterville, New York, as the 'Corporal John P. Sigsbee Post Office.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 337 West Clark Street in Eureka, California, as the 'Sam Sacco Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Veterans and Military Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving benefits and support for veterans and military families.

1: Makes disabled veterans with severe burn injuries eligible for automobile and adaptive equipment assistance.
2: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act to extend emergency unemployment compensation payments for eligible individuals
Appropriate funds out of the employment security administration account to assist states in providing reemployment activities.
3: Directs the Social Security Administration to make a one-time cost-of-living increase of 3.9% to benefits under title II of the Social Security Act.
4: Prohibits terminating the eligibility of an individual who qualifies for treatment as a dependent under TRICARE solely by reason of exceeding the age limit of 21 years
Requires the continuation of coverage for individuals dependent on the member for over one-half of the child's support.
5: Directs the Secretary of the military department concerned to support the provision of funeral honors for veterans through a daily stipend.
6: Directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to jointly establish a program of treatment and rehabilitative services for traumatic brain injury
Requires the Secretary of Defense to establish protocols for the detection and diagnosis of TBI.
7: Revises provisions concerning eligibility for military retired pay for nonregular service
Continues 60 as the minimum eligibility age for such retirees for health care.

topic.name: Health Initiatives for At-Risk Populations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving health care services and preventive measures for underserved and at-risk populations, including minorities, college students, and vulnerable families.

Document 1: grants to public and nonprofit private health care providers to provide treatment for diabetes in minority communities
public education on diabetes prevention and control
Document 2: expand coverage of screening for glaucoma to include individuals who are both older than 49 years of age and Hispanic
target screening activities to populations primarily consisting of high-risk individuals
Document 3: improve mental and behavioral health services and outreach on college and university campuses
target underserved and at-risk populations
Document 4: prevent suicides among all ages, particularly among groups that are at high risk for suicide
enhance services for students with mental health or substance use disorders at institutions of higher education
Document 5: provide training to graduate medical residents in preventive medicine specialties
financial assistance to resident physicians who plan to specialize in preventive medicine or public health
Document 6: review VA policies for maintaining health care quality and patient safety at VA medical facilities
recruit and retain VHA physicians in hard-to-fill positions
Document 7: promote the well-being of maltreated infants and toddlers and their families
prevent the recurrence of child abuse and neglect

topic.name: Education Innovation and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing educational practices and support systems within K-12 schools.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to include training of school personnel to recognize child sexual abuse
uses of grants to states and subgrants to local educational agencies
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to establish a program to encourage the interest of female students in STEM careers
prepares female students to pursue credentials needed to pursue a career in a STEM field
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to replace the program providing incentive payments to public elementary schools for foreign language instruction
provides matching grants to partnerships composed of state or local educational agencies and institutions of higher education
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Education to establish the Empowering Children with Autism through Education Task Force
identify and disseminate evidence-based educational strategies to improve the quality of learning for individuals with autism
Document 5: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow local educational agencies to use state subgrants to develop programs promoting safe internet use by students
educate students about appropriate online behavior
Document 6: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to support school innovations
requires each grant to be used to address areas of school innovation

topic.name: Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: The documents focus on various aspects of land management, conveyance, and preservation involving federal, state, and tribal interests.

Document 1: Designates certain land at Point Spencer in Alaska to be retained by the Coast Guard; conveyed to, or leased by, the state of Alaska; and conveyed to the Bering Straits Native Corporation.
Requires the Secretary to develop a joint management plan for the use and management of the lands retained, conveyed, or leased under this Act.
Document 2: Redesignates the Ocmulgee National Monument in Georgia as the Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park.
Directs the Department of the Interior to conduct a special resource study of the Ocmulgee River corridor to determine the national significance of the study area.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Directs the Secretary to acquire, for the benefit of the Tribe, trust title to 200 acres of land within the Tribe's service area.
Document 4: Designates specified Bureau of Land Management (BLM) land in Oregon as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Withdraws federal land within the wilderness areas from all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to Jefferson County, Montana, the parcel of National Forest System land known as the Elkhorn Cemetery.
Requires the county to manage the cemetery with due consideration and protection for the historic and cultural values of the cemetery.
Document 6: Expands the boundaries of the Cibola National Forest in New Mexico to include the Manzano Wilderness Study Area.
Requires the Manzano Wilderness Study Area to be designated as wilderness and added to the Manzano Mountain Wilderness.
Document 7: Requires the Bureau of Land Management for a state to select an eligible federal lands parcel for conveyance in response to a request by an adjacent landholder.
Bars a conveyed eligible parcel from exceeding 160 acres unless the BLM or the Forest Service approves a request for additional acreage.

topic.name: Small Business Procurement and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting and supporting small business ownership and procurement opportunities, particularly for women, service-disabled veterans, and disadvantaged groups.

Document 1: Establishes the Commission on Women's Business Ownership
review and make recommendations concerning the status of women business owners
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Commerce to ensure that all members of the Armed Forces deployed abroad are fully and accurately counted
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to subject to certain penalties any person misrepresenting the status of a business owned by service-disabled veterans
Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to establish an Office of Service Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business Procurement
Document 4: increases the government-wide small business procurement contract goal
the government-wide procurement goal for small disadvantaged businesses and women-owned businesses
Document 5: extends the authority for Small Business Administration refinancing of small business debt
Document 6: makes small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible for small business development grants
Document 7: requires each Office of Small and Disadvantaged Business Utilization of the SBA to assist small business concerns awarded a federal contract
make available on the SBA website a list of resources for small business concerns seeking education and assistance

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming and regulating U.S. immigration policies and procedures.

Document 1: H1B Strengthening Anti-Fraud Effectiveness Act
authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security
Document 2: Prohibits...an alien who on May 8, 2008, was a resident of the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands
Makes such an alien parent eligible for a special nonimmigrant visa
Document 3: Border Security Enforcement Act of 2011
deploy at least 6,000 National Guard personnel to perform operations in the Southwest Border region
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to allow an alien widow of a U.S. citizen
self-petition for continued immediate relative status
Document 5: Immigration Backlog Reduction Act of 2011
expedited removal of inadmissible arriving aliens
Document 6: initial 36-month admission period for a nonimmigrant alien employed as a dairy worker under an H-2A visa
Document 7: Defense of Legal Workers Act of 2015
exclude from the definition of 'unauthorized alien'

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing criminal justice, public safety, and victim protection in the United States.

Document 1: Drug Trafficking Safe Harbor Elimination Act of 2011 - Amends the Controlled Substances Act
subject to the same penalties that would apply to such conduct if it were to occur within the United States.
Document 2: Sex Offender Registration Tips Program Act of 2009
provide grants and access to information and resources to Parents for Megan's Law, Inc.
Document 3: Gun Show Background Check Act of 2008 - Amends the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act
require registration of gun show promoters and to set forth the responsibilities of promoters.
Document 4: Preventing Victims of Stalking Act of 2013 - Amends the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act
prohibit the sale or other disposition of a firearm or ammunition to any person knowing or having reasonable cause to believe that such person is subject to a court order.
Document 5: Military Domestic and Sexual Violence Response Act - Establishes in the Department of Defense (DOD) an Office of the Victims' Advocate
to facilitate access to services for victims of domestic or family violence, sexual assault, and stalking in the military.
Document 6: Matthew Shepard Local Law Enforcement Hate Crimes Prevention Act of 2007 - Authorizes the Attorney General to provide technical, forensic, prosecutorial, or other assistance
in the criminal investigation or prosecution of any crime that constitutes a crime of violence under federal law.
Document 7: Refuge from Cruel Trapping Act - Amends the National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966
to prohibit the use or possession of body-gripping traps in the National Wildlife Refuge System.

topic.name: Federal Funding and Procurement Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to federal funding mechanisms, procurement regulations, and the allocation of resources for various state and federal programs.

Document 1: Allows a state to elect not to participate in the federal-aid highway program
Requires the state making an election to agree to maintain the Interstate System
Document 2: Amends the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) to reauthorize and make appropriations for the grant program
authorizes and makes appropriations for FY2012-FY2015
Document 3: Directs the U.S. Postal Service to issue a semipostal to benefit the Peace Corps
Document 4: establishes a Flag Office Revolving Fund for the Architect of the Capitol
reimbursement of any appropriations account for the salaries of employees of the Flag Office
Document 5: Revises requirements for the equity bonus program for specified federal-aid highway programs
Increases from 92% to 105% the percentage for such a state of the total fiscal year apportionments
Document 6: Prohibits funds from being obligated or otherwise made available for an item listed in the joint explanatory statement
rescinds earmarks designated in such Act
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to certify annually to Congress
Prohibits the head of each executive agency from awarding a contract for the procurement of Chinese goods

topic.name: Federal Agency Accountability and Efficiency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving accountability, efficiency, and transparency in federal agencies and their operations.

Document 1: Veterans Health Care Budget Reform Act of 2008 - Requires a two-fiscal-year budget authority for various accounts of the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Conducts a study on the adequacy and accuracy of VA baseline model projections for health care expenditures.
Document 2: Government Customer Service Improvement Act of 2012 - Directs the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to develop performance measures for federal agencies.
Requires each federal agency to designate a service improvement officer.
Document 3: Bureau Examination Fairness Act - Prohibits the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau from including enforcement attorneys when examining entities under its jurisdiction.
Requires the CFPB to produce a report of examination or supervisory letter within 120 days after the examination is completed.
Document 4: Bonuses for Cost-Cutters Act of 2016 - Expands the cash awards program for disclosures by federal employees of fraud, waste, or mismanagement.
Agencies must submit reports to the Department of the Treasury regarding total savings achieved through disclosures.
Document 5: Report the Costs Act - Requires annual reports of federal agency chief financial officers to include the cost of multilingual services provided.
OMB must issue guidance for agency chief financial officers in compiling reports on multilingual services.
Document 6: Requires federal agencies to submit a report to Congress before hosting a conference, detailing costs and purposes of the event.
Defines 'conference' as a meeting involving travel for 50 or more attendees.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the FDA to evaluate the use of evidence from clinical experience for drug approvals.
FDA must establish a draft framework for data collection from clinical experience.

topic.name: Military Construction and Appropriations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to military construction projects, appropriations for defense operations, and the establishment of military medical facilities.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to disestablish the Joint Task Force National Capital Region Medical.
Requires such office to assume responsibility for the development and implementation of the comprehensive master plan to provide sufficient world-class military medical facilities.
Document 2: Makes continuing appropriations for FY2012.
Prohibits the use of appropriations, funds, or authority granted under this Act for the Department of Defense (DOD) for new production of items not funded for production in FY2011.
Document 4: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2006 to authorize special immigrant status for certain Afghan or Iraqi translators.
Authorizes additional FY2007-FY2008 entrants (up to 500 annually).
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Air Force to construct additions and alterations to the jet engine shop at Holloman Air Force Base.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Army to implement the First Sergeants Barracks Initiative (FSBI) throughout the Army.
Document 7: Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012 - Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to acquire real property and carry out military construction projects.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension and Amendments
topic.description: The documents primarily discuss the temporary suspension of duties on various goods and amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend the duty on certain narrow woven fabrics.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on Mixtures of N-Y2-(2-oxoimidazolidine-1-yl)ethyl?-2-methylacrylamide, methacrylic acid, aminoethyl ethylene urea and hydroquinone.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain textile device covers and stands.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to modify textile and apparel rules of origin.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Methyl (E)-methoxyimino-2(2-o-tolyloxymethyl) phenyl) acetate.
Document 6: suspend the duty on certain lamps used in liquid chromatographs or spectrophotometry.
Document 7: authorize the President to designate certain luggage and travel articles as eligible for duty-free treatment.

topic.name: Regulatory Amendments in Health and Safety
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative amendments aimed at regulating health, safety, and security standards across different sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to remove dihydrocodeinone from classification as a schedule III controlled substance.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to establish systems to inspect cargo to ensure the security of all cargo transported in domestic or foreign all-cargo aircraft.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Labor to promulgate an interim final standard regulating occupational exposure to combustible dust hazards.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem genetically-engineered fish to be unsafe under provisions related to new animal drugs and adulterated food.
Document 5: Requires the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to conduct security screening at airports that lost commercial air service.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require an abbreviated application for a new drug containing certain antibiotics.
Document 7: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder' and regulate their practices.

topic.name: Offshore Energy Development
topic.description: Legislation and policies related to the exploration and production of energy resources, particularly oil and natural gas, from the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands from leasing for natural gas exploration and production.

Prohibits the Secretary from issuing a lease for natural gas exploration or development in any OCS area located more than 50 miles and less than 100 miles from the coastline of a state if the state has enacted a law disapproving such lease issuance.
Document 2: Requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to establish a direct loan program for high risk geothermal exploration wells.
DOE must establish a program of research, development, demonstration, and commercial application for geothermal heat pumps and the direct use of geothermal energy.
Document 3: Declares as having no force or effect federal prohibitions against the expenditure of appropriated funds to conduct oil and gas leasing and preleasing activities for areas of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) of the United States adjacent to Cuba.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior promptly to conduct lease sales for such locations on the OCS in accordance with the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act.
Document 4: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas in any area of the outer Continental Shelf off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington.
Document 6: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to authorize the governor of Virginia to petition the Secretary of the Interior for authorization to conduct natural gas or crude oil exploration and extraction activities.

Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit into a Clean Energy Fund of 50% of all rentals, royalties, bonus bids, and other sums due and payable to the United States from leases entered into under this Act.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various legislative efforts aimed at reforming campaign finance laws in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to subject to its requirements as a political committee any applicable 527 organization.
Sets forth rules for allocation and funding by a political committee for certain expenses relating to federal and non-federal activities.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements and benefits of fair elections financing of Senate election campaigns.
Establishment of a Fair Elections Fund.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require a corporation that submits regular, periodic reports to its shareholders to include in each such report specified information on disbursements it has made for certain political activity.
Denies a tax exemption for a social welfare organization if its expenditures for the taxable year for covered political activity exceed the lesser of 10% of its total expenditures or $10 million.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to candidate benefits of fair elections financing of House of Representatives election campaigns.
Establishment of the Fair Elections Fund and of a Fair Elections Oversight Board.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to allow the use of contributions or donations only for the six years following the most recent federal election for a six-year office.
Requires contributions or donations remaining available to such an individual after such six-year period ends to be disposed of within 30 days.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments and Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts that amend the Internal Revenue Code to provide various tax benefits, credits, and adjustments for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an adjustment for inflation in tax credit for dependent care expenses.
exclusion from gross income for employer-provided dependent care assistance.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the excise tax rate on distilled spirits.
Makes the $2.70 per proof gallon rate applicable to a specified controlled group of corporations.
Document 3: Renders the general terminating date of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 inapplicable.
extended the exclusion of employer-provided educational assistance from the gross income of employees.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the Hope Scholarship and Lifetime Learning tax credits.
increases the income threshold for reductions in the credit amount based upon modified adjusted gross income.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt employers from social security taxes for new employees.
allow an increase in the general business tax credit for the retention of such employees.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the new markets tax credit through 2014.
provide for an inflation adjustment to the limitation amount for such credit.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employees in the building and construction industry to make distributions from their tax-exempt employer pension plans at age 55.
participants in such plan on or before April 30, 2013.
